description: Task-type labels describing what the student is required to do in a data structures or algorithms exam question.

label_policy.primary_definition: The main intellectual output expected from the student.
label_policy.secondary_definition: Supporting reasoning required to justify or analyze the primary output.

labels.name: Design Data Structure
labels.description: Construct or propose a data structure supporting specified operations and constraints (e.g., worst-case or amortized bounds).

labels.name: Design Algorithm
labels.description: Design a deterministic algorithm to solve a computational problem within specified time/space constraints.

labels.name: Design Randomized Algorithm
labels.description: Design an algorithm that uses randomness to achieve expected time/space bounds or solve a problem.

labels.name: Prove Correctness
labels.description: Prove or rigorously justify that an algorithm or data structure is correct or maintains its invariants.

labels.name: Analyze Complexity
labels.description: Analyze asymptotic time and/or space complexity (worst-case, average, expected, or amortized) of given operations or algorithms.

labels.name: Exact Bound and Counting
labels.description: Compute exact, non-asymptotic bounds or count specific operations (e.g., exact number of linkings, comparisons, node heights, or tree splits).

labels.name: Probabilistic Analysis
labels.description: Analyze the expected case, probability of specific events (e.g., collisions in hashing, partition sizes), or average-case complexity for randomized algorithms.

labels.name: Amortized Analysis
labels.description: Prove amortized bounds using aggregate, accounting, or potential methods for sequences of operations.

labels.name: Solve Recurrence
labels.description: Solve or analyze a recurrence relation to find an asymptotic bound.

labels.name: Asymptotic Reasoning
labels.description: Reason about asymptotic relationships, growth rates, or Big-O/Theta/Omega laws between functions.

labels.name: Prove Lower Bound
labels.description: Prove a lower bound or impossibility result under a specified model (e.g., comparison model).

labels.name: Evaluate Feasibility
labels.description: Determine if a specific time/space complexity or structural property is mathematically possible to achieve, and prove/disprove it.

labels.name: Construct Counterexample
labels.description: Disprove a claim by constructing a concrete counterexample or specific sequence of operations.

labels.name: Trace or Simulate Execution
labels.description: Simulate or trace the execution of an algorithm or data structure on a given input to determine intermediate states or final output.

labels.name: Augment Existing Data Structure
labels.description: Modify or extend an existing data structure (like adding fields to AVL or Red-Black trees) while preserving performance guarantees.

labels.name: Select Answer and Justify
labels.description: Choose correct option(s) in multiple-choice or true/false format, often requiring a brief mathematical or logical justification.
